lesson.menu_desc: Modal Verbs: should, could, might -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 10 -- Meetings That Matter -- Running a Team Meeting
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Walyson Ginaldo Silva | Aula 10 -- Meetings That Matter

listenings.text: Hi team, this is a quick reminder about tomorrow's project meeting at ten o'clock. Please read the agenda before you arrive, because we have a lot to cover. Every attendee should come prepared with a short update. Walyson will chair the meeting this time, and he will take the minutes. We should start with the budget, because we need to allocate it carefully this quarter. After that, we could brainstorm ideas for the new launch. The client might join us for the last ten minutes, so please be ready to give a clear recap. If we reach consensus early, we could adjourn before lunch. Thank you, and see you tomorrow.

This is my recap of today's project meeting. I chaired the meeting for the first time, and I took the minutes. We followed the agenda closely. First, we discussed the budget, and we agreed that we should allocate more money to marketing. Then we had a short brainstorm about the new launch, and Maria shared a strong proposal. We could not reach consensus on the date, so we might postpone the launch by two weeks. I allocated three action items: I will send the minutes, Carlos will contact the client, and Maria will prepare the numbers. We adjourned on time. Overall, I feel the meeting went well.

extra_audio.text: First, share the agenda with every attendee. Next, start the meeting and take the minutes. After that, discuss each point and brainstorm ideas. Then, allocate the action items. Finally, recap the decisions and adjourn the meeting.
